mappings.rationaleAI: Atmospheric science covers humidity (A.1, A.1.a), condensation (A.2.a), clouds (A.3.a-c), precipitation (A.4, A.4.b, A.4.c), storms (B.1-B.4), and forecasting (C.1-C.4) including El Nino (C.4.a) and predictability limits (C.4.b). Climate factors include temperature (E.1.b) and pressure (E.1.c).

Climate system covers factors (E.1), greenhouse effect (E.1.a.ii), albedo (E.1.a.iii), oceans (E.1.d), moisture (E.1.e), El Nino (E.2.a), human activities (E.2.c), climatic types (E.3), terrestrial life (E.4), and change (E.6).
Anthropogenic warming addresses human activities on climate (E.2.c). Ecological disruption addresses terrestrial life (E.4). Evidence addresses climatic change (E.6, E.6.a).
Atmospheric composition addresses moisture (A.1). Aerosols connect to condensation nuclei (A.2.b). Greenhouse effect addresses E.1.a.ii. Heat transport addresses E.1.
Carbon cycling addresses greenhouse effect (E.1.a.ii) and climate factors (E.1). Anthropogenic disruption addresses E.2.c. Snowball Earth addresses E.6.

Storms address winds and storms (B). Thunderstorms address B.2. Severe weather addresses B.3. Tropical cyclones address B.4. Flooding addresses precipitation effects (A.4.d). Global heating addresses E.2.c.
Solar brightness changes address solar radiation (E.1.a) and variability of the solar constant (E.1.a.i).
Sensitive dependence on initial conditions addresses limits to predictability (C.4.b).
Quaternary ice age addresses climatic change (E.6) and glaciation evidence (E.6.a).
Desert types address climatic types (E.3). Rainfall distribution connects to A.4.c. Local terrain effects address E.2.b. Microclimates address E.5.
Humanity's climate impact addresses effects of human activities on climate (E.2.c).
Mountain weather patterns address local terrain effects (E.2.b). Mountain microclimates address E.5.
Species distribution patterns address influences of climate on terrestrial life (E.4).
Ocean climate regulation addresses oceans as climate factor (E.1.d). Water cycle role connects to moisture cycle (E.1.e).
Glaciers and ice sheets address climatic change (E.6). Glacial landscapes address evidence (E.6.a).
